loopStyle: Event-driven TUI poll loop (crossterm/ratatui); the model runs on a background thread and streams LlmEvents drained each tick, and EVERY tool call is human-gated y/n/a/q before it runs

nodes.label: User sends message
nodes.note: In the Chat screen, **Enter** appends a user message to the session (**add_message**) and calls **send_llm_message()** (app.rs:1348). There is no separate agent loop — the TUI render loop (**main.rs:69**) is the driver.

nodes.label: provider.chat → stream thread
nodes.note: **send_llm_message** (app.rs:934) builds the system prompt + history, attaches the **6 tool definitions** (provider/mod.rs:127), and calls **provider.chat(...)** which spawns a background thread returning a **Receiver<LlmEvent>**. waiting_for_response is set; the loop does **not** block.

nodes.label: check_response drains LlmEvents
nodes.note: Each tick of the render loop (**main.rs:81**) calls **check_response**, which try_recv's one event: **Text** → append to the assistant buffer; **ToolUse** → queue; **Done** → branch on pending work; **Error** → abort. This poller is the real loop.

nodes.label: ToolUse → queue + gate
nodes.note: A **ToolUse** event pushes (name, input) onto **pending_tool_calls** and, unless **auto_approve_tools** is set (app.rs:529 — the sticky 'All' choice), flips **awaiting_tool_confirmation** and shows `Waiting for confirmation: <tool> [Y]es [N]o [A]ll [Q]uit` (app.rs:546).

nodes.label: y/n/a/q confirmation gate
nodes.note: While **awaiting_tool_confirmation**, handle_input routes **every** keystroke to **handle_tool_confirmation** (app.rs:1210): **y/Y** → confirm; **n/N** → reject; **a/A** → approve-all + sticky auto-approve; **q/Q/Esc** → cancel the whole queue. This is a real, mandatory, per-call gate — maximal approval, zero autonomy by default.

nodes.label: Run tool (home-sandboxed)
nodes.note: **confirm_tool_execution** pops the queue and calls **execute_tool** (app.rs:789), dispatching to one of read/write/edit/glob/grep/bash (tools.rs). Every filesystem tool **canonicalizes the path and refuses anything outside $HOME** (e.g. tools.rs:136); bash refuses to run unless cwd is under $HOME (tools.rs:615). The result string is queued in pending_tool_results.

nodes.label: Tool rejected by user
nodes.note: **reject_tool_execution** pops the call and records the literal result `"Tool execution rejected by user"`, then **advance_tool_queue** (app.rs:713). The refusal is fed back to the model as the tool's result — the loop continues rather than ending.

nodes.label: Feed results back to model
nodes.note: Once the queue drains and the **Done** event has arrived (app.rs:554 sets done_received, app.rs:608 / process_tool_completion at app.rs:729 fire), **continue_with_tool_results** saves the assistant + tool-result messages and calls **provider.continue_with_tools** — another model generation with the results appended (provider/mod.rs:101).

nodes.label: Final response saved
nodes.note: A **Done** event with **no pending tool calls or results** saves the assistant message (add_message_full, app.rs:618), clears the buffer, and sets **waiting_for_response = false** (app.rs:633) — the turn ends and the poller goes idle until the next user message.

scenarios.title: Model answers with **no tools**
scenarios.note: provider.chat streams **Text** events into the buffer; the **Done** event finds no pending tool calls or results, so the final assistant message is saved and waiting_for_response clears. The system prompt explicitly tells the model **not** to use tools for casual conversation (app.rs:1010).

scenarios.title: User asks to **edit a file**
scenarios.note: The model emits an **edit** ToolUse; the call queues and the **[Y]es [N]o [A]ll [Q]uit** prompt appears. Pressing **y** runs edit (home-sandboxed, exact-match replace, tools.rs:242). The result is fed back via continue_with_tool_results, the model re-prompts, finishes with no further tools, and the turn ends.

scenarios.title: **Tool call is denied** (press n)
scenarios.note: At the gate the user presses **n**. reject_tool_execution records `"Tool execution rejected by user"` as the tool result and the queue advances. That refusal is sent back to the model (continue_with_tool_results), which then responds without the tool and the turn ends. (Pressing **q** instead would abort the whole turn straight to done — the cancel-queue edge.)

scenarios.title: Model issues **several tool calls**
scenarios.note: Multiple **ToolUse** events queue in **pending_tool_calls** (a VecDeque). Each is gated **separately** — y/n decided per call — and advance_tool_queue (app.rs:713) re-shows the prompt for the next one. Pressing **a** once would auto-approve the rest of the queue and all future calls this session. After all run, the collected results are fed back and the model finishes.
